questions.q: Which Pandava killed the rakshasa Bakasura?
options: Arjuna
Yudhishthira
Bhima
Sahadeva
questions.explanation: Bhima, sent by Kunti in place of their host's family, fought and killed the man-eating demon.

questions.q: Near which town did Bakasura exact his tribute of food and human lives?
options: Ekachakra
Varanavata
Kampilya
Hastinapura
questions.explanation: Bakasura preyed on Ekachakra, the small town where the Pandavas were hiding in disguise after the lac house fire.

questions.q: What did Bakasura's regular tribute consist of?
options: A herd of cattle and a cask of wine
A cartload of food, two buffaloes, and the person who brought them
A chest of jewels and ten servants
A boatload of grain and a fisherman
questions.explanation: The demon devoured the cartload of food, the two buffaloes that drew it, and the human who delivered it.

questions.q: Who decided that Bhima would deliver the tribute to Bakasura?
options: Yudhishthira
The brahmin host
The town elders
Kunti
questions.explanation: Kunti volunteered Bhima to spare their host's family, trusting completely in her son's strength.

questions.q: What kind of being was Bakasura?
options: A serpent king of the underworld
A man-eating rakshasa
A fallen gandharva
A shape-shifting yaksha
questions.explanation: Baka was a powerful rakshasa, a man-eating demon who had terrorized the region around Ekachakra for years.

questions.q: What did Bhima do with the cartload of food meant for the demon?
options: He sat down and ate all of it himself
He distributed it to the town's poor
He hid it in the forest as bait
He offered it to the gods in sacrifice
questions.explanation: Bhima calmly devoured the entire tribute before the demon's eyes, turning the offering into an insult.

questions.q: How did Bhima finally kill Bakasura?
options: He crushed him under the tribute cart
He strangled him with his bare hands
He broke the demon's back
He drove a tree trunk through his chest
questions.explanation: After a long grapple, Bhima bent the demon double and snapped his back, killing him.

questions.q: Where was Bakasura's corpse found the morning after the fight?
options: In the demon's forest cave
On the steps of the temple
In the middle of the marketplace
At the gate of the town
questions.explanation: Bhima dumped the giant body at the town gate, where the amazed citizens discovered it like a fallen mountain.

questions.q: Whose turn to provide the demon's victim prompted Kunti to volunteer Bhima?
options: The brahmin family sheltering the Pandavas
The potter who lived next door
The headman of the town
The priest of the village temple
questions.explanation: The lot had fallen on the very family hosting the disguised Pandavas, and Kunti chose to repay their kindness.

questions.q: Why did the townspeople keep paying Bakasura's terrible tribute?
options: A curse bound them to obey the demon
In return, the demon protected the town and spared the rest of its people
The king had ordered it as punishment
They believed the demon was a god in disguise
questions.explanation: The pact bought safety, for Baka kept enemies and other demons away and stopped slaughtering the townsfolk at random.

questions.q: How did Bakasura first attack when he found Bhima devouring his tribute?
options: He hurled a boulder at the cart
He breathed fire across the clearing
He seized the buffaloes and flung them aside
He struck Bhima's back with both arms from behind
questions.explanation: The enraged demon roared and pounded Bhima's back with both arms, yet the blows did not interrupt the meal.

questions.q: How did Bhima respond to the demon's first furious blows?
options: He went on calmly eating without even turning to look
He leapt up and grappled with him at once
He hurled the empty cart at the demon
He taunted the demon with the story of Hidimba
questions.explanation: Bhima refused to be hurried, finishing every last morsel of the tribute before rising to fight.

questions.q: Once the food was finished, how did the fight between Bhima and Bakasura unfold?
options: They duelled with mace and club until midnight
They fought in the air, the demon changing shapes
They tore up trees to hurl at each other, then closed in to grapple
They wrestled in the river until the demon tired
questions.explanation: Demon and Pandava uprooted trees and flung them at one another before the struggle ended in a deadly wrestling hold.

questions.q: What duty did Kunti say the Pandavas would fulfil by sending Bhima with the tribute?
options: The kshatriya duty of obeying a king's command
Repaying the shelter and kindness of their host
Fulfilling a vow she had made to the gods
Honoring a promise given to Vyasa
questions.explanation: Kunti insisted that gratitude to the brahmin who had sheltered them demanded that they save his family from the demon.

questions.q: What promise did Bhima extract from Bakasura's kinsmen after the slaying?
options: To carry the Pandavas wherever they wished
To guard the town against bandits
To stop killing humans and trouble the town no more
To send tribute to the town each spring
questions.explanation: The dead demon's frightened kin swore to give up preying on people, ending the rakshasa menace around Ekachakra.

questions.q: How was the demon's death explained to the curious townspeople?
options: The host said a high-souled brahmin skilled in mantras had volunteered and slain him
The elders declared the gods had answered the town's prayers
A hunter claimed credit and was richly rewarded
The king's minister announced his soldiers had done it
questions.explanation: Bound to secrecy by Kunti, the brahmin host credited an unnamed mantra-knowing brahmin who had offered to deliver the tribute.

questions.q: Before Kunti intervened, what had the members of the host family been arguing over?
options: Whether to flee the town under cover of night
Whether to beg the king for soldiers
Whether to offer the demon double food instead of a person
Which one of them should be the one to go and die
questions.explanation: The brahmin, his wife, and their daughter each insisted on going to the demon, while the infant son's brave babble made them smile through tears.

questions.q: How did Bhima announce himself when he reached the demon's haunt?
options: He blew a great blast on a horn
He called out Baka's name while eating the demon's food
He set fire to the edge of the forest
He drove the cart straight into the demon's cave
questions.explanation: Bhima shouted the rakshasa's name in challenge and then sat down to enjoy the tribute meant for him.

questions.q: What did Bhima do immediately after leaving the demon's body for the town to find?
options: He woke the town to proclaim the victory
He waited at the gate for the people to gather
He returned unseen to the brahmin's house and quietly told his family
He set off alone toward Panchala
questions.explanation: Bhima slipped back unnoticed, and the Pandavas kept their part in the demon's death a strict secret.

questions.q: From whom did Kunti hear the full story of the town's pact with the demon?
options: The brahmin host, after she pressed him about his family's grief
A washerwoman at the river
The town's headman at the well
Bhima, who had heard it in the market
questions.explanation: Hearing the family weeping, Kunti questioned the brahmin, who poured out the tale of Baka's tribute and their doomed turn.

questions.q: What had life in Ekachakra been like before the tribute arrangement was struck?
options: The town had been guarded by a garrison of the king
The demon had slept for a century and harmed no one
The town had paid its tribute in cattle alone
The rakshasa had raided the town and devoured people at his whim
questions.explanation: Baka used to kill indiscriminately, so the desperate citizens negotiated the fixed tribute to make his predation predictable.

questions.q: Why was there no king to protect the town from Bakasura?
options: The king had been killed by the demon years before
The region's ruler was feeble and far away, offering no defense
The throne was disputed between rival princes
The king had made a secret alliance with the demon
questions.explanation: The brahmin lamented that their distant, weak ruler gave the town no protection, leaving it to bargain with the demon alone.

questions.q: Which rakshasa, called a brother of Baka, attacked the Pandavas in the Kamyaka forest at the start of their exile and was also slain by Bhima?
options: Alambusha
Kirmira
Jatasura
Alayudha
questions.explanation: Kirmira, described as Baka's brother and Hidimba's friend, blocked the exiles' path in Kamyaka and was killed by Bhima in the Kirmira-vadha episode.

questions.q: Which kinsman of Baka came to the Kurukshetra war seeking revenge and was beheaded by Ghatotkacha on the fourteenth night?
options: Alayudha
Jatasura
Kirmira
Hidimba
questions.explanation: Alayudha demanded the chance to avenge Baka and his slain kin, and Ghatotkacha killed him in the great night battle.

questions.q: According to the brahmin's account, what happened to households that tried to evade their turn to feed Baka?
options: The town council exiled them
The demon spared them if they fasted
The demon devoured the entire family
The king's men seized their property
questions.explanation: Defaulting on the tribute brought the rakshasa's full wrath, with the whole family eaten in place of the one victim.

questions.q: What two gains did Kunti cite when Yudhishthira protested her decision to send Bhima?
options: A reward of gold and the town's lasting friendship
A safe refuge and a marriage alliance
Vyasa's blessing and the demon's treasure
Repaying their host's kindness and earning great merit by saving the town
questions.explanation: Kunti argued the deed would at once discharge their debt of hospitality and win the high merit of rescuing many lives.

questions.q: What does the name Baka literally mean in Sanskrit?
options: Crane
Bear
Thunderbolt
Cauldron
questions.explanation: Baka means crane or heron, a name shared by other figures such as the crane demon slain by the child Krishna.

questions.q: Where did the absentee ruler of the region around Ekachakra hold court, according to the brahmin's lament?
options: Girivraja
Mahishmati
Vetrakiya
Pundravardhana
questions.explanation: The brahmin said the king dwelt far off at Vetrakiya, called Vetrakiyagriha in the text, and gave the town no protection.

questions.q: How does the epic describe the moment of Bakasura's death?
options: Beheaded by a single sweep of Bhima's sword
Bent and broken over Bhima's knee, vomiting blood as his back snapped
Hurled into the river and drowned
Burned in the fire of his own cave
questions.explanation: Bhima seized the exhausted demon, pressed him down, and broke his body, and Baka died roaring with blood pouring from his mouth.

questions.q: What did Kunti ask of the brahmin host once the demon was dead?
options: To leave Ekachakra with the Pandavas
To dedicate a shrine to the unknown hero
To send word of the deed to Hastinapura
To tell no one who had really killed the rakshasa
questions.explanation: Protecting her sons' cover was everything, so Kunti bound the grateful family to silence about Bhima's deed.
